name.text: Calculate and Print Two Circle Areas

questiontext.text:
Write a Python program that creates the following variables:

  • pi = 3.14159
  • radius1 = 10
  • radius2 = 12

Then calculate:

  • area1 = pi * radius1 * radius1
  • area2 = pi * radius2 * radius2

Print area1 on the first line and area2 on the second line.

Expected output:

314.159
452.38896

generalfeedback.text:
Common mistakes for this question include:

  • Typing the wrong value for pi, radius1, or radius2
  • Using addition instead of multiplication when calculating the area
  • Forgetting that the radius must be multiplied by itself
  • Printing the variable names instead of their values
  • Printing the answers in the wrong order

question.answerpreload:
# Create the variables below pi = 3.14159 radius1 = 10 radius2 = 12
# Calculate the first area here area1 =
# Calculate the second area here area2 =
# Print the answers here print(area1) print(area2)
question.template:
import contextlib import io import json
student_code = """{{ STUDENT_ANSWER | e('py') }}""" stdout_buffer = io.StringIO() checks = [ ("Check the first printed line: area1", "314.159", 0, 1.5), ("Check the second printed line: area2", "452.38896", 1, 1.5), ]
try: compiled = compile(student_code, "<student>", "exec") with contextlib.redirect_stdout(stdout_buffer): exec(compiled, {"__name__": "__main__"})
output_lines = stdout_buffer.getvalue().splitlines() rows = [["Check", "Expected", "Got", "Comment", "Mark", "iscorrect"]] total_awarded = 0.0
for label, expected, target_index, mark in checks: if len(output_lines) <= target_index: got = "(missing output line)" comment = f"Expected line {target_index + 1}, but your program printed only {len(output_lines)} line(s)." awarded = 0.0 iscorrect = 0 else: got = output_lines[target_index] if got == expected: comment = f"Line {target_index + 1} is correct." awarded = mark iscorrect = 1 else: comment = f"Line {target_index + 1} is incorrect." awarded = 0.0 iscorrect = 0
total_awarded += awarded rows.append([label, expected, got, comment, f"{awarded:.1f}/{mark:.1f}", iscorrect])
fraction = total_awarded / 3.0 except Exception as exc: rows = [[ "Check", "Expected", "Got", "Comment", "Mark", "iscorrect" ], [ "Program execution", "Program runs successfully", f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", "Your program did not run successfully.", "0.0/3.0", 0 ]] fraction = 0.0
print(json.dumps({ "fraction": fraction, "testresults": rows }))

testcode.text: Run the program and grade each printed line separately.
